     The Numbers Business Report

Smallfoot (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $83,240,103Details
International Box Office $134,700,000
Worldwide Box Office $217,940,103
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $11,083,635
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $10,883,274
Total $21,966,909
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A Young yeti finds something he thought didn’t exist—a human. News of this “smallfoot” throws the simple yeti community into an uproar over what else might be out there in the big world beyond their snowy village, in an all new story about friendship, courage and the joy of discovery.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$23,045,635 (27.7% of total gross)
Legs:3.61 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:38.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$80,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,131 opening theaters/4,131 max. theaters, 5.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $109,279,297

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 28th, 2018 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: September 21st, 2018 (Wide), released as Yeti: Ledové dobrodružství (Czech Republic)
September 21st, 2018 (Wide), released as Piadinôžka (Slovakia)
September 21st, 2018 (Wide) (Australia)
September 27th, 2018 (Wide), released as Pie Pequeño (Chile)
September 28th, 2018 (Wide) (Poland)
Video Release: December 4th, 2018 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG for some action, rude humor, and thematic elements.
(Rating bulletin 2537 (Cert #51715), 7/25/2018)
Running Time: 96 minutes
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D
Plot point: Bigfoot, Falsely Accused, Humans as Monsters, Mountain Climbing
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Zaftig Films, RatPac Entertainment, Warner Bros., Warner Animation Group
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A Young yeti finds something he thought didn’t exist—a human. News of this “smallfoot” throws the simple yeti community into an uproar over what else might be out there in the big world beyond their snowy village, in an all new story about friendship, courage and the joy of discovery.
